name: BLUE BIRD BAKERY
subtitle: Artisan Bakery · Patisserie
tagline: Sourdough baked before dawn, patisserie by mid-morning, and coffee worth crossing town for.
pageTitle: BLUE BIRD BAKERY — Artisan Bakery in York

services.name: Sourdough & Bread
services.desc: Slow-fermented country loaves, rye, and seeded batons — baked fresh every morning.

services.name: Patisserie
services.desc: Croissants, pains au chocolat, and seasonal tarts made by hand before the shop opens.

services.name: Celebration Cakes
services.desc: Birthday, wedding and occasion cakes, designed with you. Two weeks' notice please.

services.name: Coffee & Cake
services.desc: Single-origin espresso, flat whites, and a slice of the day's cake to sit or take away.

services.name: Lunch & Savouries
services.desc: Quiches, sausage rolls and filled focaccia for a proper lunch on the go.

services.name: Wholesale
services.desc: Bread and pastries supplied to cafés and offices across York. Ask about a weekly order.

about.title: A small bakery with a long rise.
paragraphs: Blue Bird Bakery has been baking in York since 2014 — sourdough before dawn, patisserie by mid-morning, and a coffee worth crossing town for.
Everything is made on site using Yorkshire flour and slow fermentation. If it isn’t good enough for our own breakfast table, it doesn’t go in the window.
We work with local growers and a roaster up the road, because the short chain tastes better — and it’s the right thing to do.

whyChoose.text: Naturally leavened — long fermentation, no improvers, proper flavour
Baked fresh every morning — if it's left from yesterday, we don't sell it
Local & independent — Yorkshire flour, a roaster up the road, no factories
510+ five-star reviews — read them on Google before you visit

reviews.name: Lucy Bennett
reviews.text: Best sourdough in York, bar none. The seeded baton is my weekly ritual.

reviews.name: Mark Fletcher
reviews.text: Ordered a wedding cake — stunning, and it tasted even better than it looked.

reviews.name: Aisha K.
reviews.text: Coffee and a croissant on a Saturday morning. Always warm, always worth the queue.
